I had never experienced anything like it before.

My whole body was shaking, and it felt absolutely magical.

The crowd was screaming, the reef was detonating beneath one of the heaviest waves on the planet, and for a few moments everything else disappeared.

Teahupo’o had been a dream for as long as I can remember. Standing there, witnessing its power with my own eyes, was something I’ll never forget.

And to make it even more surreal, it was the biggest day I’ve ever paddled out to film from the water.

Some places exceed every expectation. Teahupo’o is one of them.

Life has a funny way of redirecting you when you need it most.

A few months ago, I picked up a camera. At first, it was just something to do—an excuse to get outside, move my feet, and clear my head. What I didn’t realize was how much it would change for me.

Photography has taken me to places I’ve never seen, pushed me to explore my own backyard, given me a reason to get out, and stay active. More importantly, it’s become a huge part of my journey to 6 months of sobriety.

Not everyone understands it. I’ve already had people doubt me, criticize me, and question why I’m putting so much into it. But every artist starts somewhere. Nobody begins at the top. We’re all beginners before we’re anything else.

For me, it isn’t just about taking pictures. It’s another creative outlet, another way to tell a story, and something I want to grow into a business.

To everyone supporting me, thank you. To anyone starting something new and feeling discouraged, keep going. The people who matter will see the work, the growth, and the passion behind it.
.

If you’re between 25–35 and starting to question your career, your income, or the direction your life is going… read this:

(In the meantime, comment “DREAM” and I’ll send you my free training where I share exactly how I made a living from my passion with Instagram)

There’s a moment no one really talks about, the moment where everything looks “fine”… but you realize it’s not what you actually want.

You followed the path that made sense, you did what you were supposed to do and you built something stable.

But somehow… it doesn’t feel like you, and the hardest part is not even knowing what you want instead. Just knowing this isn’t it.

I went through that phase too, and what changed everything for me wasn’t a big move overnight, it was a shift.
I stopped asking myself “what should I do?” and started asking myself these questions:

•  What kind of life do I actually want to wake up to every day?
•  What parts of my life am I willing to change to feel happier?
•  What passion or expertise do I have that would allow me to make more money while working fewer hours?
•  What do I already spend hours doing without realizing it?
•  What would I do if I knew I couldn’t fail?

Because at the end of the day… you’re not just building a career, you’re building a life that truly feels like you. Life is too short to spend it working a job you don’t like.

That one decision changed everything for me.

Growing up, I remember hearing the word “gay” used as an insult and believing there was something wrong with me.

I remember being as young as 10 and wishing on my birthdays that I could just be “normal.” Looking back at these photos now, I feel a lot of love for that kid.

I wish I could tell him there was never anything wrong with him.
I wish he didn’t have to carry shame for who he is.
I wish he knew just how full his life would become, and how much love and acceptance he would be met with.

Not everyone is met with that. And that’s why I’m sharing this.

Because right now, there’s still a kid sitting in a classroom, hearing those same words and wondering if there’s something wrong with them too.

This is for anyone who has ever felt different, for anyone still learning to love the parts of themselves they were taught to hide. You’re not alone. 

There was never anything wrong with you. 

Happy Pride 🏳️‍🌈

EDC LAS VEGAS: THRU MY EYES 🎇 🎆

Three years ago, I attended EDCLV as a first-time festival goer with no idea of what I was stepping into. I remember specifically walking through the raceway tunnels and coming out to see a panoramic view of what I called “Neon Disneyland” 🤣. It honestly felt like the craziesttt culture shock I had ever experienced in my life, and it made me fall in love with EDM in a way I never expected.

The more time I spent in the rave scene from that point on, the more I continued to discover the best parts of myself while simultaneously meeting some of the most incredible human beings who have genuinely lifted me up just by existing in the same space.

There’s something about EDC that never really gets old. It’s a feeling that can only be truly understood when you’re standing under the fireworks, surrounded by your favorite people in the world, and completely present in the moment… like you’re exactly where you’re supposed to be.
I have so much gratitude for the festival that gave me all of that. So much that I can love it again… and again… and again 🩵

Lake Como’s most stunning villa 😍 Villa del Balbianello is a true gem, offering incredible lake views and a stunning park 💚

📍Located in the town of Lenno, on the Como side of the Lake

🚍 Reach it by bus: From Como San Giovanni take the C110 bus (ticket made on board, say your destination is Lenno). It will take about 40 minutes (depending on the traffic).

Once in Lenno, you’ll have two options to reach the villa, a taxi boat or walking.
🚤 The taxi boat costs 10€ one way, and I believe it’s the most fascinating way to reach the villa.
🚶If you choose to walk to the villa, you can take the short route 1 km / 25 minutes or a 2.5 km / 45 minutes walk. 

🎟️ You can visit only the park (my personal recommendation) or the park and the villa.

Park only ticket is €15 
Park + guided tour of the villa €25 (the tour of the villa is interesting, as it tells the life of the last owner of the villa, and it’s nice to see the inside. But I’d personally skip it, and visit just the park)

🕣 openings vary based on the month, it’s usually closed on Monday and Wednesday. It’s open from 10 am to 6 pm on the other days. 

✅ Online booking highly recommended.

At what time to visit? I personally prefer to visit early in the afternoon. All the videos were taken between 2/3 pm. 

Have you been to Villa del Balbianello? If not, make sure to add it to your Lake Como itinerary.

The mountains have a way of making your problems feel smaller

Nature doesn’t care about your deadlines, your followers, or your plans.

Standing in front of the Three Peaks of Lavaredo, it’s hard not to feel humbled.

  It simply exists. And somehow that’s the lesson.

Personal growth rarely happens when everything is comfortable

It happens when the path gets steep and you keep going anyway

One step at a time. One conversation at a time. One lesson at a time.

The view at the top is beautiful, but who you become on the way matters more.

The older I get, the more I realize that success without peace doesn’t mean much.

Maybe that’s why I keep coming back to places like the Dolomites

They remind me that life isn’t a race. It’s an experience

The Three Peaks have been standing here for millions of years. Most of our worries won’t survive the week

Go see the world. Let nature remind you how beautiful life can be.

But as of today, we’ve used it for 3+ years, travelling almost full time, and it’s held on pretty well and we’ve always managed to travel with our fins without any trouble or extra costs 🙌🏼 

We’ve also heard that if your fins aren’t super long like ours, you could be able to use another Cressi suitcase, the Moby, but we have no personal experience with that. We also have no affiliation with Cressi, fyi 👀

We also know some people swear by taking them in as cabin luggage, and we applaud your bravery ahah 👏🏼😂 But I feel like we’ve travelled too much (or are now too old 😱) to handle that kind of stress any more 😂 It probably also depends if you travel with low cost airlines or not

Let us know if you have any interesting persona experiences to share 😍👇🏼

•

🇮🇹 🫣 Avviso:

Onestamente, non è la valigia più comoda del mondo, anzi. Purtroppo è così, è pesante e faticosa 🙈

Ma ad oggi, la usiamo da più di 3 anni, viaggiando quasi a tempo pieno, e si è comportata piuttosto bene e siamo sempre riusciti a viaggiare con le nostre pinne senza problemi o costi aggiuntivi (tra l’altro non abbiamo nessuna affiliazione con Cressi) 🙌🏼

Abbiamo anche sentito parlare bene della valigia Moby (sempre Cressi), ma devi avere le pinne un po’ più corte delle nostre 👀

Sappiamo anche che alcune persone sono convintissime di portarle come bagaglio a mano, e ammiriamo il loro coraggio ahah 👏🏼😂 Ma credo che ormai abbiamo viaggiato troppo (o siamo ormai troppo vecchi 😱) per gestire ancora questo tipo di stress 😂 Probabilmente dipende anche se viaggi con linee aeree low cost o no

A new era of @goproanz is here 🎥

Over the past few weeks, I’ve had the chance to put the all new GoPro MISSION 1 PRO through its paces. 

The biggest thing that stood out to me was the new 1” Sensor and GP3 Processor. Shooting in constantly changing light, from lowlight, to bright conditions, the camera really showed it versatility.

The new sensor and processor produce footage that feels richer, cleaner and far more flexible in post-production. Add 10-bit colour, GP-Log2, 8K60 and 8K30 Open Gate, and you’re working with footage that gives you significantly more creative freedom when it comes to grading, colours and dynamic range.

For me, the standout feature has been the low-light performance. Whether shooting before sunrise, during sunset, or in challenging low-light situations, the quality coming out of this camera is genuinely impressive.

What excites me most isn’t that it’s tougher, smaller or more waterproof than anything else. It’s the image quality. For the first time, I feel like I can take a GoPro into situations where I would previously have reached for a larger camera system.

Excited to keep putting it through its paces.

GORGONA: EL CORAZÓN AZUL DEL PACÍFICO COLOMBIANO 💙🐋🌊

Bajo la superficie de esta isla se esconde uno de los ecosistemas más extraordinarios de nuestro planeta. 🌎✨

Aquí, donde los corales mejor conservados del Pacífico occidental colombiano siguen floreciendo 🪸🌈, la vida encuentra refugio. Tiburones ballena cruzan sus aguas, tiburones de aleta blanca descansan entre los arrecifes, las morenas 🐍🌊 observan desde las grietas y cada rincón cuenta una historia milenaria. ⏳🌎

Es una sala cuna para las ballenas jorobadas 🐋 que cada año recorren miles de kilómetros para llegar a estas aguas y dar vida a una nueva generación. 🌊💕 Es un recordatorio de lo frágiles, poderosos y valiosos que son nuestros océanos. 🌎💙

Entre encuentros inolvidables ✨🦈, arrecifes llenos de vida 🪸🐠 y vestigios como el esqueleto de una tortuga 🐢💀 que alguna vez navegó estos mares, entendemos que somos simples visitantes en un mundo que merece ser protegido. 🙏
Cada inmersión aquí es un privilegio.

✨🤿 En octubre regresamos a Gorgona y aún tenemos 3 cupos disponibles para quienes sueñan con explorar uno de los lugares más mágicos, salvajes y prístinos de Colombia. 🇨🇴🌊🦈🐋

¿Te imaginas compartir el agua con tiburones ballena, arrecifes vibrantes y el sonido de las ballenas jorobadas bajo el océano? 💙🐋✨

If you’re just getting into travel videography and feel like your content is missing something, it usually comes down to the shots you choose.

These are 5 simple shots I use all the time to level up my travel videos and reels:

⚡️Establishing shot: sets the scene and shows where you are
⚡️Detail shot: captures small moments that add emotion
⚡️Walking shot: adds movement and makes the viewer feel present
⚡️Push-out shot with movement: reveals the scene while making your shot feel more dynamic
⚡️Orbit shot: adds depth and makes a moment feel more cinematic

📨Save this for later and try them on your next trip.

Happy Father's Day. The longest bicycle journey by a father and son is 30,004.59 km (18643.98 miles), achieved by Joshua and George Kohler (UK). The duo cycled through Europe, Asia, Oceania and South America, from 29 March 2025 arriving back home in Norwich, UK on 2 May 2026. 

The adventurous pair also set records for fastest bicycle circumnavigation by a father and son (399 days, 5 hours, 52 minutes) and the most countries visited in a continuous bicycle journey by father and son (30). They took on the record challenges to bond through the experience, fundraise for @unicef, raise awareness for men's mental health and to hopefully inspire others to take on ambitious challenges. Their favourite country on their journey was China.

"We are just ordinary people, and this goes to show that ordinary people can do extraordinary things," says @joshpkohler

don’t get me wrong, i’m so grateful for everything this has turned into and every possibility that’s come from it. i just don’t want posting to start feeling like something i have to do instead of something i want to do. i want it to stay mine, my digital travel diary, honest, authentic and unhinged.

Dernière série photo de ce voyage à Komodo 🇮🇩
Je suis honnête avec toi, c’est une de mes plus belles expériences jusqu’ici dans ce magnifique pays: des paysages lunaires, des rencontres uniques et des moments de déconnexion totale 🤍
Je te partage un aperçu avec quelques anecdotes en caption sur chaque photo 📸

✨ C’est devenu notre mini-rituel du matin et aussi un de mes moments préférés: se lever tôt pour voir le sunrise sur le deck 

⛵️Pendant ce séjour, on a eu la chance d’être quasiment toujours seuls, c’était parfait pour déconnecter complètement. 

🐠 C’est une de mes rencontres préférées: les fameuses raies mantas. Je ne me rendais pas du tout compte à quel point elles sont gigantesques! 

🌿 J’avais aussi hâte de faire ce parc à ce moment de l’année parce que les paysages sont encore verts avant de tourner au orange. 

⚓️ Que ce soit sur l’eau ou sur terre, le mix était parfait entre découverte et repos. L’équipage nous a débarqué sur cette plage isolée où on a pu passer l’après-midi à bronzer. 

🐢 C’est toujours une experience unique de pouvoir nager avec une tortue: c’est incroyablement relaxant et on a eu la chance d’être avec elle un bon moment. 

⛰️ Voici un de mes points de vue préféré et aussi un des plus connus, l’île Padar. 

🌊 Fun fact: je pensais être malade en bateau mais au final ça a fait l’effet inverse, j’ai jamais aussi bien dormi! 

Notre dernier sunset, la photo parle d’elle-même je pense 🤍

Today is World Reef Awareness Day! 🪸

Often called the “rainforests of the sea,” coral reefs are beautiful, vibrant ecosystems and cornerstones of a healthy ocean. Although reefs cover less than 1% of the ocean floor, they provide critical habitat for roughly 25% of all ocean species. 

They’re also essential to human wellbeing, reducing the force of waves before they reach the shore and protecting communities from extreme weather like hurricanes. It’s estimated that reefs safeguard hundreds of millions of people in 100+ countries!

But there’s a major threat posed to reefs around the world today. This Reef Awareness Day, dive in at the link in bio to learn why ending coral bleaching starts with taking climate action! 🌊 Photo by @daniescayola.

We’re hiring and I wanted to do it differently.

I’ve been head of content production across two businesses for a while now @durackcivil in civil construction and @boldstone_ in land development, we also run a growing podcast alongside everything else.

The content we’re making is genuinely interesting. Real projects, real stories, real scale. But I need someone alongside @durackjames , @natevankan & I who can actually see that, and turn it into something people stop to watch.

I’m not looking for the most experienced person in the room. I’m looking for the most switched-on. Someone early in their career who edits like they give a damn. Who has an eye for pacing, colour, and light. Who wants to own their work, not just execute a brief.

The role is varied in the best way, construction reels, travel content, podcast edits, short-form. No two weeks the same.

If that sounds like you, send me a reel and a note. 

And if it sounds like someone you know tag them below. Seriously. The right person for this probably isn’t scrolling job boards right now. They’re in your feed.

This shark specie are mostly solitary hunters, patrolling the coral reefs, sandy lagoons, and deep channels 🪸

Despite their fearsome reputation, tiger sharks play a crucial role in maintaining the balance of marine ecosystems around the islands of Maldives 🌴

We didn’t know it then, but we’d remember this forever.

That day made history.

One year ago, we found ourselves in front of one of the most powerful and beautiful waves on Earth as Teahupo’o delivered a day that will be remembered for years to come.

The ocean was bigger than we could have imagined. Every set carried a sense of anticipation, but one wave stood above them all: Eimeo’s giant ride. A moment that instantly became part of surfing history.

Being there to witness it firsthand was a privilege. A reminder of how small we are in front of the ocean, and why we keep coming back to it.

Some days stay with you forever.

The ocean teaches patience 🩶

Everyone talks about the beach clubs. Nobody talks about the hidden beaches.

The mission was simple: find places that don’t feel like Instagram.

First stop: getting lost. Usually a good sign when traveling.

Somehow ended up at one of the most beautiful hidden beaches I’ve seen in Europe.

The kind of place you almost don’t want to share because it still feels untouched. + the back you should get 🤝♥️

Quick city stop. Needed coffee. Ended up shopping at AlphaTauri instead. @ibizagallery_thesite 

Funny how the best travel days are usually the ones you never planned.

24 hours in Ibiza and I’ve barely scratched the surface.

Maybe that’s the beauty of travel. Every destination leaves you with a reason to come back. *Werbung

Five years ago, my life looked completely different 🙈

Sometimes it’s crazy to think that one decision, one opportunity, one person can change the entire course of your life.

I’ve learned that it’s so important to listen to yourself... and to listen to the people who have already built the kind of life you dream of. What you deeply want for yourself won’t always make sense to the people around you and that’s okay. People will have expectations of who you should be, what job you should have, where you should live, what your life should look like.

But at the end of the day, you’re the one who has to wake up every morning and live with the choices you make.
So choose yourself, again and again.
